features: Core SEO, broken link, and code validation checks

pricing_plans.price: Paid subscription (exact price set by developer; billed quarterly or yearly)

enrichment.short_description: Checkr is a San Francisco-based background check platform that uses AI and automation to speed up and simplify employment screening for employers, including criminal records checks, motor vehicle records, and identity verification. Founded in 2014 by Daniel Yanisse and Jonathan Perichon, Checkr has raised over 700 million dollars in venture funding and has been valued as high as roughly 5 billion dollars, serving large gig-economy and enterprise customers like Uber, DoorDash, and Instacart.
enrichment.starting_price: Custom pricing based on package and volume; not publicly listed per-check price
enrichment.seo_title: Checkr Review 2026 - AI-Powered Background Check Platform for Employers
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Checkr is an AI-driven background check platform used by employers for criminal, MVR, and identity screening. See its history, funding, features, and pricing.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Checkr is a background check and employment screening platform that uses automation and machine learning to speed up traditionally slow, manual background-check processes for employers. Founded in 2014 by Daniel Yanisse and Jonathan Perichon and headquartered in San Francisco, Checkr built its early reputation serving the fast-growing gig-economy sector, providing background checks for companies like Uber, DoorDash, Instacart, and other platforms that needed to onboard large numbers of independent contractors quickly and at scale, a use case where traditional weeks-long background-check turnaround times were a serious bottleneck. Checkr's core technology applies natural-language processing and machine learning to court records and other data sources to standardize, categorize, and adjudicate background-check results faster and more consistently than manual review, while also building compliance tooling to help employers navigate the complex, jurisdiction-by-jurisdiction patchwork of US background-check and fair-chance-hiring laws. Its product suite has since expanded well beyond gig-economy criminal background checks into broader employment screening, including motor vehicle records (MVR) checks, employment and education verification, drug and health screening coordination, identity verification, and continuous/ongoing monitoring products that alert employers to new criminal records for existing employees rather than only checking once at hire. Checkr has raised more than 740 million dollars in venture funding across multiple rounds, including a 250 million dollar Series E in September 2021 that valued the company at around 4.6 billion dollars, followed by a further 120 million dollar Series E-II round in April 2022 that pushed its valuation to approximately 5.75 billion dollars, backed by investors including Accel, T. Rowe Price, Fidelity, Franklin Templeton, Durable Capital Partners, and Y Combinator (Checkr is a Y Combinator alumnus). It is worth noting that private-market valuations for late-stage venture companies like Checkr can shift significantly over time; as of more recent disclosures by mutual-fund investors that mark their holdings to estimated fair value, some have valued their Checkr stake well below the 2022 peak, reflecting the broader repricing of high-growth private tech companies since 2022, though Checkr has not confirmed a new formal valuation through a priced funding round. Checkr remains privately held as of the most recent available information and continues to serve a large roster of enterprise customers across gig economy, retail, healthcare, transportation, and other industries that rely on high-volume, compliant background screening.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Checkr does and how its technology works
paragraphs: Checkr's platform is built to automate the traditionally labor-intensive process of employment background screening. Rather than relying purely on manual review of court records and other data sources, Checkr applies machine learning and natural-language processing to parse, standardize, and categorize records pulled from thousands of county, state, and federal jurisdictions across the US, which helps reduce both turnaround time and the risk of inconsistent manual adjudication. Its product suite covers criminal background checks, motor vehicle record (MVR) checks for driving-related roles, employment and education verification, and identity verification, along with continuous monitoring options that notify employers if an existing employee acquires a new criminal record after their initial hire, which is particularly relevant for industries like transportation, delivery, and gig work where ongoing safety compliance matters.

long_form_blocks.heading: Funding history and valuation
paragraphs: Checkr has raised substantial venture capital as it scaled from a gig-economy-focused startup into a broad employment-screening platform. Public reporting cites a 161 million dollar Series D in September 2019, followed by a 250 million dollar Series E in September 2021 that valued the company at approximately 4.6 billion dollars, and a further 120 million dollar Series E-II extension in April 2022 that reportedly pushed valuation to around 5.75 billion dollars. Total funding raised is commonly cited at more than 740 million dollars, with backers including Accel, T. Rowe Price, Fidelity Management and Research, Franklin Templeton, Durable Capital Partners, and Y Combinator. It is worth noting for accuracy that some mutual fund investors holding Checkr shares have since marked down their internal valuation estimates for the company, with figures reported as low as roughly 368 million dollars to as high as 1.4 billion dollars in late-2024 disclosures, well below the 2022 peak; these are investor mark-to-model estimates rather than a new priced funding round, and Checkr's officially confirmed valuation remains tied to its 2022 Series E-II raise absent a subsequent public financing event.

features.title: Automated criminal background checks
features.description: Uses machine learning and NLP to process and adjudicate criminal court records faster and more consistently than manual review.

features.title: Motor vehicle record (MVR) checks
features.description: Screens driving records for roles involving vehicle operation, such as delivery, rideshare, and transportation jobs.

features.title: Employment and education verification
features.description: Confirms candidates' claimed work history and educational credentials as part of the screening process.

features.title: Identity verification
features.description: Validates candidate identity to help prevent fraud during the hiring and screening process.

features.title: Continuous and ongoing monitoring
features.description: Alerts employers to new criminal records for existing employees after their initial background check, supporting ongoing compliance.

features.title: Compliance and fair-chance hiring tools
features.description: Helps employers navigate varying state and local background-check laws, including fair-chance and ban-the-box requirements.

features.title: Candidate-facing experience
features.description: Provides candidates with a portal to track background check status and dispute inaccurate information.

features.title: API and ATS integrations
features.description: Offers APIs and integrations with applicant tracking systems so background checks can be triggered directly from existing hiring workflows.

pros: Strong specialization in high-volume, fast-turnaround screening built originally for gig-economy scale
Machine learning-driven record processing can meaningfully speed up background-check turnaround versus manual review
Broad product suite covering criminal, MVR, verification, identity, and continuous monitoring in one platform
Backed by well-known investors and substantial venture funding, indicating institutional confidence and resources
API-first design integrates cleanly into existing applicant tracking and HR systems
cons: Pricing is not publicly published and requires a sales conversation, making cost comparison difficult upfront
Private-market valuation has reportedly been marked down significantly by some investors since its 2022 peak, signaling growth-stage uncertainty
Background-check compliance requirements vary heavily by jurisdiction, which can create complexity regardless of the platform used
As with any background-check provider, turnaround time can still vary depending on the responsiveness of individual courts and data sources
Primarily built for US employment screening, with more limited applicability for international background-check needs

faqs.question: What does Checkr do?
faqs.answer: Checkr is a background check platform that uses AI and automation to help employers screen job candidates and employees, covering criminal records, motor vehicle records, employment and education verification, identity checks, and continuous monitoring.

faqs.question: When was Checkr founded?
faqs.answer: Checkr was founded in 2014 by Daniel Yanisse and Jonathan Perichon and is headquartered in San Francisco, California.

faqs.question: How much funding has Checkr raised?
faqs.answer: Checkr has raised more than 740 million dollars in venture funding, including a 250 million dollar Series E in September 2021 at roughly a 4.6 billion dollar valuation and a 120 million dollar Series E-II round in April 2022 that reportedly pushed valuation to about 5.75 billion dollars.

faqs.question: What is Checkr's current valuation?
faqs.answer: Checkr's most recent officially confirmed valuation from a priced round was approximately 5.75 billion dollars in April 2022. Some mutual fund investors have since marked their Checkr holdings down significantly, with disclosed estimates ranging roughly from 368 million to 1.4 billion dollars as of late 2024, though these are investor estimates rather than a new funding round.

faqs.question: Which companies use Checkr?
faqs.answer: Checkr is known for serving large gig-economy platforms such as Uber, DoorDash, and Instacart, alongside a broader base of enterprise customers across retail, healthcare, transportation, and other industries.

faqs.question: How much does Checkr cost?
faqs.answer: Checkr does not publish fixed per-check pricing; costs depend on the type and depth of background check package and the employer's volume, and are provided through a custom quote.

faqs.question: Is Checkr publicly traded?
faqs.answer: No. As of the most recent available information, Checkr remains a privately held company.

faqs.question: Does Checkr offer ongoing monitoring after the initial hire?
faqs.answer: Yes. Checkr offers continuous or ongoing monitoring products that notify employers if an existing employee acquires a new criminal record after their initial background check.

use_cases.title: Gig economy driver and courier screening
use_cases.description: Rideshare, delivery, and gig platforms use Checkr to run fast, high-volume background and motor vehicle record checks to onboard independent contractors quickly.
use_cases.audience: Gig economy and on-demand platform companies

use_cases.title: Enterprise employee background screening
use_cases.description: HR and talent acquisition teams at larger employers use Checkr integrated with their applicant tracking system to run standardized background checks as part of the hiring workflow.
use_cases.audience: HR and talent acquisition teams at mid-size to large employers

use_cases.title: Ongoing compliance monitoring for safety-sensitive roles
use_cases.description: Employers in transportation, delivery, and other safety-sensitive industries use Checkr's continuous monitoring to stay alerted to new criminal or driving records for current employees.
use_cases.audience: Employers in transportation, logistics, and safety-sensitive industries
